chunks.originalText:
nam2-nun-e nam2-nun-e e2-ta nam-ta-ab-ed2
den-lil2 nam2-nun-e e2-ta nam-ta-ab-ed2
nam2-nun-e nam-lugal-la e2-ta nam-ta-ab-ed2
chunks.translatedText:
The Prince, the Prince, came forth from the shrine.
Enlil, the Prince, came forth from the shrine.
The Prince, in his kingship, came forth from the shrine.

chunks.originalText:
den-lil2 kur-kur-ra igi mi-ni-in-il2-il2-i
den-lil2-ra kur ni2-ba mu-na-il2-il2-i
an ub-da 4 den-lil2-ra jickiri6-gin7 mu-na-sig7
chunks.translatedText:
Enlil lifted his eyes o'er the breadth of the lands.
The lands lifted themselves to the sight of the god.
The four bounds of heaven bloomed green like a grove.

chunks.originalText:
kecki saj il2 mu-na-ni-in-jal2
kecki kur-kur-ra saj il2-bi
den-lil2-le kecki za3-mi2 am3-ma-ab-be2
chunks.translatedText:
Kesh stood there, lifting its head to the sky.
Kesh, the head-lifter of all the lands.
Enlil spoke the praise of Kesh.

chunks.originalText:
dnisaba nu-ka-ac-bi-im
inim-bi-ta sa-gin7 im-da-an-sur
dub-ba sar-sar cu-ce3 al-ja2-ja2
chunks.translatedText:
Nisaba was the one who decided its fate.
With its words, she wove it like mesh of a net.
On tablets she wrote it, held fast in her hand.

chunks.originalText:
e2 muc3 kalam-ma gud huc aratta
e2 kecki muc3 kalam-ma gud huc aratta
chunks.translatedText:
House, crown of the land, fierce bull of Aratta.
House Kesh, crown of the land, fierce bull of Aratta.

chunks.originalText:
hur-saj-da mu2-a an-da gu2 la2-a
e2-kur-da mu2-a kur-ra saj il2-bi
abzu-gin7 ri-a hur-saj-gin7 sig7-sig7-ga
chunks.translatedText:
Grown with the mountain, embracing the sky.
Grown with Ekur, the peak of the world.
Founded like Abzu, green as the hills.

chunks.originalText:
kecki-gin7 rib-ba lu2 ci-in-ga-tum2-mu
ur-saj-bi dacac7-gi4-gin7 rib-ba ama ci-in-ga-u3-tud
nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
e2 1-kam-ma
chunks.translatedText:
Will any man bring forth a wonder like Kesh?
Will any mother bear a hero like Ashgi?
Who has seen a lady as great as Nintud?
The first house.
